old_claim.note: Social Stratification provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No social-stratification edge remains because large communal construction demonstrates coordination and labor investment but does not by itself establish ranked social classes as a technical prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Executive Summary pp. 3-5 describes the wall, ditch, tower, and the social and economic structures needed to manage communal construction without identifying stratified classes as necessary.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Tell es-Sultan evidences organized communal building, while the source does not establish social stratification as its prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Coordination can occur under multiple social arrangements, so labor scale cannot be converted into a specific hierarchy node without independent evidence.

ontology_before: Monumental labor investment was equated with an already developed stratified social order.
ontology_after: The architectural record retains communal coordination without encoding a disputed social hierarchy as technical causation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_fortifications no longer directly depends on social_stratification.
would_reject_if: Direct evidence established ranked social classes as necessary organizers of the PPNA construction project.
short_reason: Communal construction does not by itself prove social stratification.
